fields.description:
## Why

The completion verifier (`src/rebar/llm/completion.py`, 295 LOC) is a direct single-agent call
with a deterministic child-closure/signature precheck, reconciliation, and citation downgrade —
OFF the workflow engine. It is the SIMPLER of the two gate migrations (the walking skeleton): it
needs NO new constructs, only existing step kinds. Migrating it first derisks the workflow-as-gate
approach for the rebar maintainers/engine before the larger plan-review migration (B2), and gives
a uniform, observable, individually-testable gate.

## What

Author a v3 engine workflow (e.g. `.rebar/workflows/completion-verification.yaml`) expressing the
verifier with existing step kinds. The shape mirrors `completion.py`'s real control flow — which is
NOT linear: the child-closure precheck SHORT-CIRCUITS (completion.py:223-225 returns a deterministic
FAIL verdict and SKIPS the LLM, no billable call). So:

1. `uses` **precheck** — deterministic child-closure + signature check; emits a `precheck_failed`
   boolean AND, on failure, the synthesized deterministic FAIL `completion_verdict`.
A `branch` on `steps.precheck.outputs.run_verify` models the short-circuit (referencing an
if:-SKIPPED step's outputs raises, so a branch — whose arms reference only steps that run in
them — is the faithful construct):
2. THEN arm (precheck passed): `prompt` **verify** — the agentic `completion-verifier` reviewer
   (ONE tool-using call, emits a `completion_verdict`; engine auto-injects ticket_id/ticket_context/
   repo_path) → `uses` **reconcile** — enforces FAIL⇔findings + downgrades hallucinated citations.
3. ELSE arm (precheck failed): pass through precheck's deterministic FAIL `completion_verdict`
   WITHOUT calling the LLM (behaviour + cost preserved exactly as completion.py:223-225).

The deterministic stages wrap the existing `completion.py` helpers (`_child_closure_findings` /
`_deterministic_child_failure` / the reconcile + citation-downgrade helpers) WITHOUT changing their
logic. The workflow PRODUCES a `completion_verdict`; it does NOT sign — `completion.py` has no
signing helper (signing lives in the close-gate / `verify_signature` path), so signing is deferred
to the B5 live cutover. This story is purely additive (expand-contract: the existing `completion.py`
call path stays the live gate; cutover/removal is B5), so B3 is reversible.

## Scope

- The `completion-verification` workflow YAML + the `uses` ops wrapping the precheck / reconcile
  helpers (extracted from `completion.py` without behavioural change), with the `prompt` verify step
  conditionally skipped on precheck failure via `if:`.
- Reference the existing `completion-verifier` prompt + `completion_verdict` schema.
- A short design note / ADR capturing the workflow-as-gate pattern (uses → conditional prompt →
  uses) for reuse by B2.
- Runs end-to-end OFFLINE (FakeAgentRunner); the existing `completion.py` path is untouched.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A v3 `completion-verification` engine workflow expresses precheck (`uses`) → a `branch` on the
      precheck result whose THEN arm runs agentic verify (`prompt`) → reconcile (`uses`) and whose
      ELSE arm passes through the deterministic FAIL verdict — so a failed precheck SKIPS the LLM
      with no call; it produces a `completion_verdict` and does NOT sign (signing is deferred to B5).
- [ ] The deterministic stages wrap the existing `completion.py` helpers (child-closure+signature
      precheck, FAIL⇔findings reconciliation, citation downgrade) with no change to their logic; the
      existing `completion.py` call path is left intact (expand-contract; cutover is B5).
- [ ] A golden-fixture parity test confirms the workflow produces the same `completion_verdict` as
      the existing `completion.py` call for representative fixtures (PASS and FAIL), making "without
      behavioural change" observable.
- [ ] Failure-path/invariant tests assert: (a) a failing child-closure precheck short-circuits —
      the `prompt` step is skipped (no LLM call) and the deterministic FAIL verdict flows through;
      (b) reconcile enforces FAIL⇔findings; (c) an unsupported file citation is downgraded.
- [ ] The workflow validates against the v3 schema and the linter reports no errors; a short design
      note records the workflow-as-gate pattern for B2.
- [ ] `make lint` + the full test suite pass.
